title: Philosopher
description: One philosopher = one JSON file at data/philosophers/{id}.json. k and centrality are DERIVED (Stage 3-4), never hand-filled. All Chinese text MUST be Traditional (繁體); CI rejects simplified characters.

id.description: Canonical key = Wikidata QID. Semantic-free, decoupled from name (decision: id locked to Q{QID}).

slug.description: Human-readable URL/display slug. NOT a stable key — derived from name_en, may change.

name_zh.description: 繁體中文 only. CI runs a simplified-character detector and blocks any simplified glyph. null when no Traditional-Chinese name is established (留白勝過填錯).

tradition.description: Primary intellectual lineage / canon pool the figure is studied under (not birthplace).

centrality.description: DERIVED from k (Stage 4). k>=2 -> core. contested (1<=k<2) and adjacent are below K_MIN and do not appear in the atlas. Never hand-filled.

review.description: true when k==2.0 (provisional core, single non-Wikidata vote away from threshold). DERIVED.

birth_year.description: BCE is negative (e.g. Socrates = -469). Integer for timeline comparison.

date_certainty.description: DERIVED from Wikidata P569 timePrecision: >=9 (year/month/day) -> exact; 7-8 (century/decade) -> approximate; missing -> uncertain.

active_regions.description: Map pins. Decision B = multiple precise residences. Each region is a real Wikidata geographic claim (birth P19 / death P20 / work P937 / residence P551). Exactly one region has primary=true (the default map position across the lifespan).

period.description: [start_year, end_year]. Only filled when Wikidata carries date qualifiers (P580/P582); otherwise null (留白勝過填錯). The primary/birth region defaults to the lifespan for scrubber visibility.

anchor.description: What this point represents. birth=P19, death=P20, work=P937, residence=P551.

source_property.description: Wikidata property this point was derived from (audit trail).

primary.description: True for the single default map position (the birth anchor). Frontend may override to a work region when the scrubber year falls inside its period.

polity_source.description: Provenance of polity_sequence. 'curated' = hand-verified (exact dates). 'historical-basemaps' = derived by point-in-polygon against aourednik/historical-basemaps time-slices (CC-BY-SA); dates are snapshot-approximate and entity names are dataset-native. null = no polity_sequence.

polity_sequence.description: Polity history of THIS location (chronological). Never a present-day-country label. Empty = not yet researched (留白), NOT 'unknown polity'. Dates are exact when polity_source=curated, snapshot-approximate when historical-basemaps.

polity.description: Historical political entity name (e.g. 'Kingdom of Prussia'). No modern-sovereignty labels.

membership_sources.description: The k evidence chain — the ONLY legitimate source of k and centrality. Empty -> CI warning.

entry_type.description: dedicated=1.0, canon_list=1.0, topic=0.5. Same source, highest type wins.

raw_name.description: The name string as it appeared in the source (audit trail).

works.description: Notable works from Wikidata P800. Independent of k (works do NOT affect inclusion or centrality).

title.description: English/original title (falls back to Wikidata QID if unlabelled).

title_zh.description: 繁體中文 title if available.

works_sources.description: Sources that verify works[] (Wikidata P800; ctext deferred pending API key). Do NOT count toward k. Separate channel.

k.description: DERIVED: sum of distinct source weights. Atlas inclusion requires k >= K_MIN (2.0).

sitelinks.description: Wikidata sitelink count (notability proxy, English-biased — do not use to silently drop non-Western figures).

description.description: One-line descriptor from Wikidata (schema:description). zh-hant preferred, opencc-converted zh, else en fallback.

bio.description: Intro paragraph from zh Wikipedia (opencc -> Traditional, trimmed ~600 chars). CC-BY-SA. null = no zh Wikipedia article (留白).

bio_source.description: Provenance of bio. Currently only zh Wikipedia.

description_en.description: One-line English descriptor from Wikidata (schema:description@en). For bilingual/English mode.

bio_en.description: Intro paragraph from en Wikipedia (trimmed ~700 chars). CC BY-SA. null = no en Wikipedia article.
